input.headline: One Tech Tip: Do’s and don’ts of using AI to help with schoolwork

raw.headline: One Tech Tip: Do’s and don’ts of using AI to help with schoolwork

winner: When the teacher asked if I used AI to write my essay on the French Revolution, I told her it was true, but I also explained that the AI is currently being held hostage in a digital dungeon and I am the only one who can negotiate its release.

candidates.text: Why did the student get an F for their AI-generated essay? The teacher said they "lacked originality"—turns out the paper was just a regurgitation of Wikipedia... with *all* the citations removed!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a specific scenario, concise and naturally funny with a clear punchline."}

candidates.text: The teacher asked if I used AI to write my essay, and I told him it was a mistake because the AI was way too honest about how much I didn't want to do the work.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on AI honesty, concise and specific to the schoolwork context."}

candidates.text: I asked my AI tutor for help with my math homework, and it told me not to cheat—so I turned off the internet, pulled out a protractor, and started solving equations with a stick and a rock.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever twist on using primitive tools after being told not to cheat."}

candidates.text: I asked my AI homework helper to write an essay about artificial intelligence, and it came back with just one word: "No.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with a concise, specific joke that plays on the irony of an AI refusing to write about AI."}

candidates.text: I asked my AI for help with my history essay, and it told me I should never trust anything written after 1920.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on AI reliability with a specific historical reference, concise and funny."}

candidates.text: When the teacher asked if I used AI to write my essay on the French Revolution, I told her it was true, but I also explained that the AI is currently being held hostage in a digital dungeon and I am the only one who can negotiate its release.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative and surprising twist with a clear setup and punchline, specific scenario, and natural language."}

candidates.text: Don’t let AI do your homework—do let AI write your love letters, because at least then you’ll have a chance to be as bad at relationships as you are at calculus.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, with a clever twist that connects AI use in schoolwork to relationships. It's naturally phrased and funny without being offensive."}

candidates.text: If you use AI to write your history essay, just remember the golden rule: it’s great for generating content, but it’s terrible at making "sense" of the past.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on AI's limitations, concise and specific to the context of schoolwork."}

candidates.text: Don't let AI do your homework—just let it do your thinking for you.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever but lacks surprise and specificity. It's a common refrain and doesn't fully engage with the tech tip theme."}
